name: import-engine subsystem deep drill
description: Deep drill of import-engine subsystem: 8 modules (~950 LOC), 7 test files, all parsers accept file paths (not buffers), throw on failure (no Result type), KML/GPX are the only parsers that can return empty arrays without throwing. GeoPackage is the most complex (WASM SQLite + GP binary header parsing). Clean extraction from web inline parsers — no copy-paste debt.

name: drawing-tools-subsystem-drill
description: Drawing Tools subsystem deep drill: DrawingToolbar owns Terra Draw behavior (finish handler, mode switching, feature removal), MapEditorState owns lifecycle (init/stop). Three operational modes via callback props: normal draw, measurement, region capture. Old 3-store + bridge architecture fully replaced by atomic methods on MapEditorState class. No endorheic basins. Noted issues: duplicate Escape handlers, double removeFeatures in error path, undo closures capture layer by reference.

name: map-canvas-subsystem-deep-drill
description: Map Canvas subsystem deep drill: 960-line component with dual rendering paths (GeoJSON + Martin VT), deck.gl heatmap bypass, queueMicrotask workaround for Svelte 5 effect depth limit, viewport sync via viewportVersion counter cycle prevention, layerRenderCache memoization preventing infinite paint-reference loop. hotOverlay ownership ambiguous (utils/ but MapCanvas-scoped). 35 boundary crossings (27 inbound, 8 outbound). Docs at docs/architecture/subsystems/map-canvas/

name: Layer Management Subsystem Deep Drill
description: LayerPanel owns CRUD+visibility+reorder, StylePanel owns style editing with dual-store rollback pattern (styleStore+layersStore in tandem). Optimistic concurrency via version columns. Create/delete are non-optimistic; toggle/reorder/style are optimistic with rollback. Reorder CONFLICT deliberately not reverted client-side (trade-off after past rollback bugs). 4 endorheic basins identified (all low-medium risk, memory-only).

name: Dual-Store Style Rollback
description: StylePanel writes to BOTH styleStore (ephemeral overrides) and layersStore (canonical objects) on every edit. On save failure, both revert to lastSavedStyle snapshot. MapCanvas resolves style as styleStore.getStyle(id) ?? layer.style. Tested in style-panel-rollback.test.ts.

title: Reorder CONFLICT non-revert
rationale: When layers.reorder fails with CONFLICT, the client store retains the optimistic z-order rather than reverting. Past rollback bugs in reorder were worse than stale-until-reload. Self-resolves on page reload. Subsequent reorders will also fail (wrong versions) but feature operations are unaffected.
